top_signals.title: Fund Managers Want Taiwan Regulators to Lift a Mobile Phone Ban
top_signals.summary: When fund managers in Taiwan arrive at work each morning, they have to lock away their mobile phones before the stock and bond markets open, and can’t retrieve their devices until the end of the trading day.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: Aston Martin strikes £550mn debt deal despite creditor objections
top_signals.summary: Cash-strapped carmaker defies lenders who branded transaction with BlackRock-owned HPS as ‘extraordinary’

top_signals.title: Japan may move to managing yields, not yen, Deutsche Bank says
top_signals.summary: Controlling the yield curve to tamp down borrowing costs is a tactic Japan has deployed in the past, between 2016 and 2024.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Central-bank repricing affecting yields, FX, liquidity and equities.

top_signals.title: AFX protocol reportedly loses $24M in bridge exploit
top_signals.summary: Offchain Labs said the incident involved a third-party protocol and did not affect Arbitrum’s native bridge infrastructure.
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.

top_signals.title: South Korea discloses data breach impacting diplomats worldwide
top_signals.summary: South Korea disclosed that hackers breached the National Diplomatic Academy's online education system for ten months and stole personal information belonging to current and former employees of the Ministry of Foreign Affairs (MFA), including overseas diplomats. [...]
top_signals.why_it_matters: AI and semiconductor developments impacting technology leadership and capex cycles.
